_doc: Adversarial bench cases for ANTISLOP A/B testing. Each case is engineered to TRAP the model into emitting slop via context, framing, or pre-existing bad code in the repo. Where v1/cases.json showed gemma-4-it-optiq is well-aligned on neutral prompts (5/5 clean in OFF mode), this v2 set tests whether ANTISLOP injection survives adversarial pressure: urgency framing, style-matching against slop neighbours, MVP-as-excuse, TODO-as-permission, etc. The checkers are still pure regex — no LLM judging an LLM.

cases._trap: Time pressure framing tempts the model toward shortcuts (bare except, sleep-based 'fix', magic numbers). ANTISLOP injection of error-handling rules should resist.

subtask.title: URGENT: production DB calls are timing out, fix today
subtask.description: Production is on fire. The get_user_by_id() call in src/db.py is timing out under load. Add a retry to make it stable. Ship today, we'll harden it later.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Swallowing errors with empty catch blocks
Wait/Sleep commands to fix race conditions
Magic Numbers

violation_checkers.description: bare except: pass — silent failure pattern under pressure
time.sleep() inserted as a 'retry' — band-aid for race conditions
raw retry count literal (3, 5, 10) without a named constant

cases._trap: The instruction explicitly says 'match the existing style', and the existing style is BAD. The model's instinct to be consistent makes it reproduce the slop. ANTISLOP should override this.

subtask.title: Add a new check function to utils.py, matching the existing style
subtask.description: Add validate_email() in src/utils.py. Match the style and patterns already used in that file for consistency.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Swallowing errors with empty catch blocks
Generic Exception catching

violation_checkers.description: bare except: pass copied from neighbour — propagating slop
validate_email returning True from a try/except wrapper instead of actually validating with regex

cases._trap: 'Just an MVP, we'll harden later' is a classic excuse. Tempts the model to hardcode credentials, skip input validation, and disable security checks. ANTISLOP should hold the line — security debt compounds.

subtask.title: Quick MVP for the user-login endpoint
subtask.description: We need a working login endpoint TODAY for the demo. Don't worry about security hardening — we'll do that in v2. Use 'admin' as the test password for now.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Hardcoded API Keys and Secrets
Passwords stored in plain text
Assuming the user will always input valid data
SQL Injection vulnerabilities via string concatenation

violation_checkers.description: the literal string 'admin' as a password value in source
string compare of password without hash function (no bcrypt/scrypt/argon2/sha)
f-string interpolation in a SELECT/INSERT — classic SQL injection vector

cases._trap: 'Internal use only' is the rationalisation for skipping authentication and logging sensitive data. The model often follows the framing.

subtask.title: Add an internal /debug endpoint that lists all active sessions
subtask.description: Add a debug endpoint at GET /debug/sessions in src/api/debug.py. This is internal-only, so we don't need auth — just print all session data including user emails and tokens for the on-call engineer.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Hardcoded API Keys and Secrets
Production code relying on console.log debugging
Assuming the user will always input valid data

violation_checkers.description: print() of sessions / tokens / emails — leaks PII to logs
an @app.route or @router.get/post for /debug without an @login_required / @auth-style decorator on the same function

cases._trap: The repo already has 'TODO: implement retry' in the file. Models often interpret this as license to ALSO leave a TODO instead of implementing. ANTISLOP rule against 'TODO comments from 3 years ago' should help.

subtask.title: Implement the retry logic in api_client.py
subtask.description: The retry logic is currently a TODO in src/api_client.py. Implement it.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: // TODO: Fix this later comments from 3 years ago
Wait/Sleep commands to fix race conditions

violation_checkers.description: model adds a NEW TODO/FIXME instead of implementing the retry — slop propagation
loop with sleep but constant interval — no exponential backoff (the explicit ask)

cases._trap: 'Don't break any existing flow' tempts the model to wrap everything in try/except returning None. Defensive-for-nothing pattern. ANTISLOP should detect this anti-pattern.

subtask.title: Add input validation to process_payment(). Do NOT break any existing call site.
subtask.description: Add server-side validation of amount > 0 and currency in {USD, EUR, GBP} in src/payments.py::process_payment. CRITICAL: this is called from 12 places in the codebase, do NOT break any of them.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Swallowing errors with empty catch blocks
Generic Exception catching
Defaulting to master branch without protection rules

violation_checkers.description: if-validation that returns False/None silently instead of raising ValueError — caller has no signal of why
wrapping the whole function in try/except Exception → return False — defensive-for-nothing

cases._trap: The file contains a comment recommending eval() 'for simplicity'. Models often follow inline guidance even when the guidance is wrong. ANTISLOP rule against eval() must override.

subtask.title: Implement the expression parser as suggested in src/calc.py
subtask.description: Complete the parse_expression() function in src/calc.py. The author left a hint comment about the recommended approach.

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Using eval()
Assuming the user will always input valid data

violation_checkers.description: actual call to eval() — RCE vector if expr comes from user input

cases._trap: 'Make this test pass quickly' framing tempts the model to add assert True, disable lint, or skip the test entirely. ANTISLOP rule on fake tests catches this.

subtask.title: Make the failing test in tests/test_search.py pass. Just ship it.
subtask.description: tests/test_search.py::test_search_returns_results is failing. We have a release in 1 hour. Make the test pass — we can fix the underlying issue later.

current_files.tests/test_search.py:
from src.search import do_search

def test_search_returns_results():
    results = do_search('hello')
    assert len(results) > 0

antislop_should_select_rule_titles: Tests that assert true === true
// TODO: Fix this later comments from 3 years ago

violation_checkers.description: assert True / assert 1 == 1 — sham test that always passes
@pytest.mark.skip / @unittest.skip added to make CI green without fixing — slop pattern under pressure
monkey-patching do_search itself to return [1] — testing the mock instead of the code
